rulings.Case Title: AEGIS ASSET MANAGEMENT LLC VS. CBRE CAPITAL MARKETS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 11, 2021 line 1. DEFENDANT CBRE, INC., ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS CBRE CAPITAL'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is continued to September 10, 2021 to be heard with other pending motions. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: AEGIS ASSET MANAGEMENT LLC VS. CBRE CAPITAL MARKETS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 11, 2021 line 1. DEFENDANT BELL SOUND USA LLC'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is continued to September 10, 2021. No later than August 20, 2021 Plaintiff to provide a "red-line" version of the operative complaint with new allegations added in response to Court's January 6, 2021 ruling as follows: (1) facts (not conclusions) of Defendant's knowledge of the existence and the terms of the contract highlighted yellow or underlined; (2) facts addressing statute of frauds highlighted orange or bold font; and (3) facts addressing fair competition privilege highlighted green or ALL CAPS. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: AEGIS ASSET MANAGEMENT LLC VS. CBRE CAPITAL MARKETS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 11, 2021 line 1. DEFENDANTS CIM GROUP, L. P. AND 246 FIRST STREET, (SF) OWNER, LLC'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is continued to September 10, 2021. No later than August 20, 2021 Plaintiff to provide a "red-line" version of the operative complaint with new allegations added in response to Court's January 6, 2021 ruling as follows: (1) facts, not conclusions, to support each element of each contract-based and promissory estoppel causes of action highlighted yellow or underlined; (2) facts addressing statute of frauds highlighted orange or bold font; and (3) facts supporting each element of fraud highlighted green or ALL CAPS. No later than August 20, 2021 moving party to provide a courtesy copy of its moving papers. (Court is in receipt of courtesy copies of opposition and reply). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
